betaBanner.lead: HairAudit is currently in public beta.
betaBanner.detail: All patient reviews are free during our public beta. Findings are checked by reviewers when needed. Clinics and doctors participating in beta receive free access and early ranking placement.

footer.brandBlurb: Independent, evidence-based reviews of hair transplant procedures.
footer.tagline: HairAudit — Audit and feedback for hair transplant procedures
footer.ecosystemBandTitle: Part of the Surgical Intelligence Ecosystem™
footer.crossPlatformHairAuditMessage: HairAudit is powered by the Follicle Intelligence engine.
footer.crossPlatformFiMessage: HairAudit is the first application powered by Follicle Intelligence.

signup.privacyReassurance: Your photos and details stay private and are never published without your permission.

home.title: HairAudit | Global Quality Assurance for Hair Restoration
home.description: The global quality assurance platform for hair restoration — measure surgical quality, verify outcomes and build trust through structured evidence. Part of the Follicle Intelligence Network.

howItWorks.title: How HairAudit Independent Analysis Works | Patient Protection | HairAudit
howItWorks.description: How HairAudit delivers independent analysis: secure photo intake, Clinical Intelligence Reports, confidence-aware reporting, and patient protection—without selling surgery.

professionals.title: For Professionals | Independent Hair Transplant Audit Standards | HairAudit
professionals.description: Methodology, scoring, evidence standards, and participation frameworks for clinics and professionals seeking transparency, QA, and benchmarking—aligned to HairAudit’s independent forensic audit model.

sampleReport.title: Sample Hair Transplant Audit Report | HairAudit
sampleReport.description: Proof of HairAudit report output: scorecard by domain, donor and recipient analysis, technique and design notes, confidence limits, and plain-language next steps—marketing walkthrough of example content; submit your own case via Request a review.

postOpHairProtectionGuide.title: Long-Term Hair Restoration Guide | Patient Education | HairAudit
postOpHairProtectionGuide.description: HairAudit's long-term hair restoration guide helps patients protect grafts, understand growth timelines, monitor progress, and enrich future reviews—unlocked after you submit your free case.

shared.reviewAfterSubmitLi1: We check your photos and timeline for completeness.
shared.reviewAfterSubmitLi2: Your photos and timeline are structured for careful clinical review.
shared.reviewAfterSubmitLi3: A clinical reviewer verifies findings before your report is released.
shared.reviewAfterSubmitLi4: You receive clear next-step guidance in plain language.

patientTrust.independentBody: HairAudit is separate from your clinic. We do not sell surgery or paid clinic placements.
patientTrust.privacyBody: Your photos and details stay private. Nothing is published without your clear permission.
patientTrust.notMarketingBody: This is an evidence report, not a sales page. Findings follow what your photos support.
patientTrust.humanReviewBody: Structured scoring is checked by reviewers when needed. Low photo quality means we say “not sure” — not a fake score.
patientTrust.secureUploadBody: Patient uploads use the same secure HairAudit flow. Use a private network you trust when you send photos.
patientTrust.notEmergencyBody: HairAudit is not urgent medical care. If you have severe pain, fever, spreading redness, or other emergency signs, seek local urgent care.

patientPhoto.title: What you will need (photos)
patientPhoto.intro: You do not need perfect photos. Add what you have now. You can add more later.
patientPhoto.itemPostOp: Right after surgery (if you have it)
patientPhoto.itemDocs: Graft count or clinic papers (if you have them)

professionalRibbon.bodyBefore: This area is for clinics, doctors, and teams building verified profiles, internal audits, and transparency records. It is separate from the patient free audit, which starts on
professionalRibbon.verification: Verification and public case display follow HairAudit contribution rules — earned through documented participation, not purchased placements.

home.heroLead: An independent, evidence-based review of your result—donor area, growth, density, hairline design, implantation, and technique—with clear, structured reporting.
home.heroSupporting: Structured review within an independent clinical framework. Not a clinic sales pitch—clarity you can use for decisions and follow-up with your treating clinician.
home.heroFootnote: Used by trichologists, surgeons, and global training academies
home.howStep1Desc: Securely upload images of your donor and recipient areas. Takes less than 2 minutes.
home.howStep2Desc: Your photos and timeline are reviewed against clinical standards for extraction quality, density, donor management, and surgical technique.
home.howStep3Desc: Get your HairAudit Score, visual breakdown, and clear next-step recommendations.
home.ecosystemDescription: HairAudit focuses on independent review, private photo handling, and plain-language reporting—separate from clinic marketing.
home.wygScoreDesc: A clear rating of your surgical outcome based on clinical standards.
home.wygDonorDesc: Assessment of extraction pattern, overharvesting risk, and donor preservation.
home.wygDensityDesc: Evaluation of graft placement, spacing, and naturalness.
home.wygTechniqueDesc: Identification of technique quality and potential red flags.
home.wygRecoDesc: Clear guidance on next steps, improvements, or corrective options.
home.sampleTeaserTitle: See What Your Results Could Reveal
home.sampleTeaserLead: Every HairAudit report provides a clear, visual breakdown of your surgery — showing what was done well and what may need attention.
home.sampleTeaserLi4: Clear clinical explanation — no confusing terminology
home.seoResourcesIntro: Explore methodology, patient guides, and common questions—then request a review when you are ready.
home.seoResourcesGraftFailure: Signs of graft failure vs shock loss
home.whyMattersP1: Results are often judged by appearance alone — but what looks acceptable can hide poor technique. HairAudit assesses what most patients cannot see: how grafts were extracted, how the donor was managed, and whether the work meets real clinical standards.
home.whyMattersP2: Not a sales tool. Independent verification of your results.
home.whoFor1: Patients who want to understand the true quality of their hair transplant
home.whoFor2: Anyone considering surgery and wanting to avoid poor outcomes
home.whoFor3: Patients who have had a transplant and are unsure about their results
home.whoFor4: Clinics and doctors who want independent validation of their work
home.privacyBody: All uploads are securely stored and handled with strict privacy controls. Your data is never shared without your consent.
home.finalCtaTitle: Get Clarity on Your Hair Transplant
home.finalCtaSubtitle: Understand your results. Identify risks. Make informed decisions.

preSurgery.title: Pre-Surgery Review
preSurgery.description: Planning a transplant? Your Pre-Surgery Review Report covers planning, suitability, graft estimate, and treatment path before you commit.

postSurgery.title: Post-Surgery Audit
postSurgery.description: Already had surgery? Your Post-Surgery Audit Report covers procedural quality, donor condition, density, areas of concern, and repair guidance.

home.certifiedSubtitle: Sample recognition certificates across tiers. Click to view the printable certificate.

patientReportValue.headline: Independent audit. Clear patient guidance.
patientReportValue.lead: HairAudit stays an independent, evidence-based review. Patient reports pair those findings with plain-language interpretation and long-term orientation—always distinct from clinic marketing or surgeon benchmarking.
patientReportValue.bullet1: Independent findings grounded in your images, timeline, and scoring criteria.
patientReportValue.bullet2: Plain-language interpretation of what the evidence supports, where confidence is limited, and why it matters.
patientReportValue.bullet3: Long-term guidance oriented to native hair health and donor protection in the context of your audit—not generic aftercare filler.
patientReportValue.bullet4: What to monitor over time so changes are noticed early and discussed appropriately with your clinician.
patientReportValue.feature1Desc: Structured conclusions tied to visible evidence and audit standards—separate from any treating clinic’s narrative.
patientReportValue.feature2Desc: Medically informed language with explicit limits: what is demonstrated, what is uncertain, and what would require additional data.
patientReportValue.feature3Desc: Durable orientation to safeguarding native density and donor reserve after surgery, framed to your case—not a one-size checklist.
patientReportValue.feature4Desc: Time-based cues for stability, regression signals, and documentation that supports informed follow-up with your medical team.
patientReportValue.guideTeaserLead: A comprehensive educational guide from Hair Longevity Institute—early healing protection, growth timelines, native hair preservation, and long-term progress monitoring for transplant patients.
patientReportValue.guideTeaserBullet1: Critical first 14 days and normal growth timeline expectations
patientReportValue.guideTeaserBullet2: Protecting native hair and understanding ongoing progression
patientReportValue.guideTeaserBullet3: Progress photography and enriching future HairAudit reviews
patientReportValue.guideTeaserBullet4: Unlocked automatically after you submit your free HairAudit case
patientReportValue.guideTeaserFootnote: Hair Longevity Institute provides the educational guide. HairAudit remains the independent audit platform. Educational content does not influence audit findings, scoring, or review outcomes.

hero.title: Long-Term Hair Restoration Guide
hero.subtitleLocked: Submit your free hair transplant case on HairAudit to access this guide. It opens as soon as your eligible case is submitted—your independent review still runs separately.
hero.subtitleUnlocked: Your guide is ready. Download the PDF below or read the full guide on this page. HairAudit remains your independent review platform; this resource is educational only.

whatItIs.title: What this guide is
whatItIs.body: A long-term patient education guide authored by Hair Longevity Institute (HLI). It helps you protect newly implanted grafts, understand normal growth timelines, monitor progress over months and years, and keep enriching your HairAudit case as your journey continues.

covers.title: What the guide covers
covers.bullet1: Critical first 14 days after surgery and early healing protection
covers.bullet2: Normal hair growth timeline and realistic expectations
covers.bullet3: Protecting native hair and understanding ongoing progression
covers.bullet4: Progress photography, monitoring, and enriching future HairAudit reviews
covers.bullet5: Educational framing only—separate from audit scoring or conclusions

whyMatters.title: Why this matters for your long-term journey
whyMatters.body: Hair restoration unfolds over months and years—not just the first recovery phase. This guide helps you protect your result, notice meaningful change early, and build a stronger long-term record for future discussions with your clinician and HairAudit.

fullGuide.lockedHint: All nine sections unlock with your PDF after submission.

access.lockedBody: Create or continue your patient case on HairAudit and submit it when you are ready. Once your free transplant case is successfully submitted, the guide unlocks here and on your patient dashboard.
access.unlockedBody: Your eligible case is on file. Save the PDF for offline reading and return here anytime to review the full guide online.

postOpHairProtectionGuide.disclaimer: Hair Longevity Institute provides the educational guide. HairAudit remains the independent audit platform. Educational content does not influence audit findings, scoring, or review outcomes.

howItWorks.heroSubtitle: A structured patient pathway from secure upload to Clinical Intelligence Report and next-step guidance.
howItWorks.afterSubmitNote: Many patients only realise something may be wrong months after surgery. HairAudit helps you understand whether your result is within expected recovery—or if independent review may help.
howItWorks.flowStep1: Start your free HairAudit pathway and choose pre-surgery review or post-surgery independent analysis.
howItWorks.flowStep2: Upload donor, hairline, crown, and timeline photos through the secure patient upload flow.
howItWorks.flowStep3: HairAudit structures your evidence for independent analysis against consistent clinical review standards.
howItWorks.flowStep4: Receive your Clinical Intelligence Report explaining evidence strength, confidence limits, and key findings.
howItWorks.flowStep5: Use next-step guidance to discuss supported findings with your clinician and plan structured follow-up when appropriate.
howItWorks.afterBody: Your Clinical Intelligence Report explains what looks well supported, where confidence is limited, and what next steps may help.
howItWorks.closingTitle: Move from uncertainty to structured evidence
howItWorks.closingSubtitle: Start Free HairAudit when you are ready, or preview a sample Clinical Intelligence Report first.

professionals.pageIntro: Built for surgeons, clinics, consultants, and independent auditors working in modern hair restoration medicine.
professionals.cardApplyDesc: Create a free clinic or doctor profile and begin with internal audits or build your verified professional presence.
professionals.cardMethodologyDesc: Structured review model, evidence weighting logic, and standardized assessment principles.
professionals.cardScoringDesc: Domain definitions, dual patient/technical explanations, and scoring clarity.
professionals.cardEvidenceDesc: Image requirements, sufficiency criteria, confidence grading, and limitations.
professionals.cardParticipationDesc: Voluntary participation pathways and quality-improvement context for clinics.
professionals.cardLegalDesc: Objective documentation principles for dispute clarification and structured review.
professionals.cardAuditorDesc: Reviewer qualifications, consistency controls, and oversight processes.

sampleReport.heroTitle: See What a Real HairAudit Report Looks Like
sampleReport.heroSubtitle: This is a structured, independent review of hair transplant quality. Every conclusion is tied to visible evidence, scoring criteria, and confidence limits so patients can make high-stakes decisions with clarity.
sampleReport.heroNote: Premium report format. Redacted sample content. No private patient data.
sampleReport.midBannerBody: Need clarity before corrective surgery or a formal complaint? Use an evidence-first audit.
sampleReport.cardOverviewDetail: Weighted scoring for planning, execution, design quality, and evidence integrity with clear confidence notes.
sampleReport.cardRadarDetail: A domain-by-domain radar profile to reveal strengths and quality gaps in one clinical visual.
sampleReport.cardImageDetail: Annotated scalp, hairline, and donor imagery with density zones, extraction patterns, and risk markers.
sampleReport.cardFindingsDetail: Structured conclusions and realistic next-step actions patients can use for follow-up, repair planning, or dispute documentation.
sampleReport.chartRadarDesc: Performance domains mapped across design, density, execution, and donor safety.
sampleReport.chartBreakdownDesc: Weighted bar chart aligned to report scoring categories.
sampleReport.meterDesc: Separates quality concerns from evidence limitations.
sampleReport.correctionVisDesc: Prioritization grid for what to address first.
sampleReport.motivation1: My result looks disappointing and I need a professional, independent quality review.
sampleReport.motivation2: I am concerned the graft count or achieved density does not match what was promised.
sampleReport.motivation3: I want an objective second opinion before committing to corrective surgery.
sampleReport.motivation4: I need structured documentation before a repair consult, complaint, or dispute.
sampleReport.footerTitle: See What Your Surgery Really Shows
sampleReport.footerBody: Move from uncertainty to structured evidence. Receive a premium independent report you can use for planning, consultations, and documentation.
sampleReport.correctionImmediateBody: Donor over-harvest risk and asymmetrical frontal density.
sampleReport.correctionNextBody: Hairline irregularity refinement and zone-specific planning.
sampleReport.correctionDocBody: Collect standardized images and operative records before any corrective commitments.
sampleReport.patientGuidanceLead: Alongside your independent review, you receive:
sampleReport.patientGuidanceLi1: Long-term preservation guidance for native hair, in context of your evidence and findings
sampleReport.patientGuidanceLi2: Donor protection education aligned with what the audit documents—not promotional reassurance
sampleReport.patientGuidanceLi3: Time-based monitoring advice to support follow-up conversations with your clinician

chrome.scoreByAreaSubtitle: Your score for each capture point (out of 5, with level)

demo.title: Clinical Intelligence Report Preview
demo.lead: See how HairAudit evaluates donor quality, progression risk, recipient viability, and planning concerns. This sample uses illustrative data only; real reports are generated from your uploaded images and details.
demo.nextStepsTitle: Ready for your own Clinical Intelligence Report?
demo.nextStepsBody: Start with secure upload. Add the photos you have now; your report will explain where evidence is strong and where confidence is limited.

html.footerLine1: HairAudit — advancing transparency in hair restoration.
html.footerLine2: This report is informational and not a medical diagnosis.

actions.noReportYet: No report yet. Run audit to generate one.
actions.useHintContext: This report can support consultation or follow-up decisions.

longTermHairRestorationGuide.coverSubtitle: Protect, monitor, and understand your hair restoration journey over time.
longTermHairRestorationGuide.footerDisclaimer: This guide is for general patient education only. It does not replace advice from your treating clinician. If you have urgent or worsening symptoms, contact your medical team promptly.
longTermHairRestorationGuide.footerIndependence: Hair Longevity Institute provides the educational guide. HairAudit remains the independent audit platform. Educational content does not influence audit findings, scoring, or review outcomes.
longTermHairRestorationGuide.educationalDisclaimer: HairAudit provides educational guidance only and does not prescribe treatment.

firstFourteenDays.title: The First 14 Days After Surgery
firstFourteenDays.purpose: Protect newly implanted grafts during the critical early healing phase.

items.elevatedSleep: Sleep with your head elevated where advised by your treating clinic.
items.avoidSweating: Avoid excessive sweating during early healing.
items.avoidExercise: Avoid strenuous exercise during early recovery.
items.avoidScratching: Avoid scratching or disturbing scabs prematurely.
items.washingProtocols: Follow washing protocols exactly as instructed by your clinic.
items.avoidTrauma: Avoid direct trauma or pressure against the transplanted area.
items.followMedication: Follow all medication instructions supplied by your treating clinician.

firstFourteenDays.closing: The first two weeks after surgery are critical for protecting newly implanted grafts while the scalp begins healing.

growthTimeline.title: Understanding The Hair Growth Timeline
growthTimeline.purpose: Reduce patient anxiety and create realistic expectations.

weeks1to2.label: Weeks 1–2
weeks1to2.description: Redness, tenderness, and scabbing are common.

weeks2to6.label: Weeks 2–6
weeks2to6.description: Temporary shedding may occur as transplanted follicles enter a resting phase.

months2to4.label: Months 2–4
months2to4.description: Minimal visible growth often occurs during this phase.

months4to8.label: Months 4–8
months4to8.description: Early growth commonly begins becoming visible.

months12to18.label: Months 12–18
months12to18.description: Final maturation may continue over an extended period.

growthTimeline.closing: Hair restoration is a gradual biological process, and early fluctuations do not necessarily indicate a poor result.

warningSigns.title: When To Contact Your Treating Clinician
warningSigns.purpose: Help patients identify abnormal recovery signs requiring prompt review.

items.increasingPain: Increasing pain rather than gradual improvement
items.spreadingRedness: Spreading redness around the treated area
items.persistentBleeding: Persistent bleeding after the early recovery phase

warningSigns.closing: If you notice unusual or worsening symptoms, contact your treating clinician promptly for direct medical review.

protectingNativeHair.title: Protecting Existing Native Hair
protectingNativeHair.purpose: Educate patients that transplanted hair does not stop surrounding hair loss progression.

paragraphs.p1: Hair transplantation restores hair that has already been lost.
paragraphs.p2: It does not stop future progression affecting surrounding native hair.
paragraphs.p3: Some patients discuss long-term preservation strategies with their doctor. Examples may include:

protectingNativeHair.safetyStatement: All medications, supplements, and treatment strategies should be discussed with your GP or qualified treating clinician before beginning treatment.

continuedProgression.title: Hair Loss Progression Does Not Always Stop After Surgery
continuedProgression.purpose: Help patients understand that surrounding native hair may continue changing over time.

paragraphs.p1: Even after transplantation, surrounding native hair may continue changing over time.
paragraphs.p2: Some patients experience continued crown thinning.
paragraphs.p3: Density imbalance may gradually develop if surrounding hair continues miniaturising.
paragraphs.p4: Long-term planning may reduce the need for future corrective procedures.

continuedProgression.closing: Hair transplantation treats visible hair loss but does not permanently stop the underlying progression of genetic hair loss.

supportiveTherapies.title: Additional Supportive Hair Therapies
supportiveTherapies.purpose: Educate patients on therapies commonly explored after surgery.
supportiveTherapies.intro: Some patients explore supportive regenerative therapies as part of broader long-term hair maintenance planning.

trackProgress.title: Continue Monitoring Your Progress
trackProgress.closing: Consistent progress photography helps monitor long-term change more accurately over time.

enrichAssessments.title: Help Improve Future HairAudit Reviews
enrichAssessments.purpose: Create a case enrichment loop for deeper future analysis.
enrichAssessments.intro: HairAudit assessments become increasingly comprehensive as more information becomes available. Future uploads may include:
enrichAssessments.closing: The more information available, the deeper future HairAudit analysis becomes.

longTermRecord.title: Your Hair Restoration Journey Continues
longTermRecord.purpose: Position HairAudit as a longitudinal record that can evolve with your journey.

paragraphs.p1: Hair restoration rarely ends with a single procedure.
paragraphs.p2: Hair characteristics may continue changing over months and years.
paragraphs.p3: Future treatments, progression patterns, and long-term maintenance decisions all contribute to long-term outcomes.
paragraphs.p4: HairAudit allows your case to evolve as new information becomes available over time.

longTermRecord.closingStatement: Your HairAudit case can continue improving as additional information, images, and long-term progress updates are added over time. The more complete your long-term record becomes, the deeper future analysis may become.

auditForms.prefilledFromSavedDefaults: Prefilled from saved defaults. Only edit what changed.
auditForms.copiedReusableDataFromPreviousCase: Copied reusable data from previous case. Only edit what changed.

auditForms.uploadImagesNextStep: Upload images in the next step.
auditForms.reviewSummaryBody: Review your answers before saving and continuing to photos.

patient.heroSubtitle: Complete your case details and photos so we can prepare your independent review.
patient.conversionPrompt: A few more details can help us give you clearer, more confident findings.

nextAction.processingBody: Your images are being carefully reviewed. We will email you when your report is ready.
nextAction.failedTitle: We were unable to complete this audit
nextAction.failedBody: Please contact support or try resubmitting your case materials.

processing.title: Your case is being reviewed now
processing.lead: Clinical specialists are working through your photos and intake details. Each step below reflects real progress — your full summary appears only when every review stage is complete.
processing.etaTypical: Most reports are prepared within a few minutes.
processing.emailConfirmed: We'll send your report link to {{email}}.
processing.emailFallback: We'll email the address on your HairAudit account when your report is ready.
processing.reassuranceClosePage: You can safely close this page — we'll email you when your report is ready.
processing.reassuranceAutoOpen: Your report will open automatically when it's ready.
processing.readyLead: Your independent case summary is prepared. You can view findings, photos, and next steps now.

timeline.photos_receivedHint: Your images are securely stored and queued for review.
timeline.organising_evidenceHint: Matching your photos and intake answers into a structured case file.
timeline.hair_pattern_reviewHint: Assessing hair loss pattern and distribution from your images.
timeline.hair_pattern_reviewDelayedHint: This step is taking a little longer than usual — your case is still in review.
timeline.donor_area_reviewHint: Reviewing donor density, coverage, and suitability markers.
timeline.donor_area_reviewDelayedHint: Donor review is still underway — no action needed from you.
timeline.restoration_suitability_reviewHint: Evaluating recipient area suitability and expected coverage context.
timeline.restoration_suitability_reviewDelayedHin: Suitability review is continuing — we'll notify you when the summary is ready.
timeline.procedural_risk_reviewHint: Checking factors worth discussing with your clinic.
timeline.procedural_risk_reviewDelayedHint: Final clinical checks are still running.
timeline.summary_preparationHint: Assembling your plain-language summary and report.
timeline.summary_preparationDelayedHint: Your summary is being finalized — this usually completes shortly.
timeline.report_readyHint: Your report link will appear here and by email.

safeSummary.title: Your review summary
safeSummary.subtitle: A calm, plain-language overview based on your uploaded images. Your full report includes additional detail by clinical area.
safeSummary.subtitleTranslated: Localized framing is shown here. This case is using the Spanish patient-safe summary translation pilot while English remains the source record.
safeSummary.subtitleTranslationUnavailable: Localized framing is shown here. The English source summary is currently shown because translated summary availability can vary by case and review status.
safeSummary.observationsHint: Expanded list of structured observations from your review. Open your full report for complete context.
safeSummary.observationsHintTranslated: These items come from the current report summary and are being shown through the bounded Spanish patient-safe summary translation pilot.
safeSummary.observationsHintTranslationUnavailable: These items come from the report summary and are currently shown from the English source version.
safeSummary.noObservations: No structured summary observations are available yet for this case.
safeSummary.concernsTitle: Areas to discuss with your clinician
safeSummary.concernsHint: These points are based on your uploaded images. They are not a diagnosis — please discuss them with your treating team.
safeSummary.disclaimer: This summary supports informed discussion with your clinic. It is not a medical diagnosis and does not replace in-person care.
safeSummary.disclaimerTranslated: Spanish translated summary text is shown only for this patient-safe pilot surface. The English source summary remains authoritative, and the full report is unchanged.
safeSummary.disclaimerTranslationUnavailable: When a translated summary is not currently available, we show the English source summary so your case information remains consistent.
safeSummary.actionPrompt: Open or download your full report for area-by-area detail and photo review.
safeSummary.trustLine: HairAudit provides an independent image-based review. Your treating clinician remains responsible for diagnosis and treatment decisions.

longTermPreservation.title: Long-Term Hair Preservation Strategy

intro.paragraph1: Hair transplantation restores areas affected by hair loss, but it does not prevent the continued progression of future thinning in surrounding native hair.
intro.paragraph2: Protecting existing hair remains one of the most important parts of long-term planning and may reduce the need for future surgery.

medical.title: Medical Hair Loss Prevention Options
medical.intro: Some patients experiencing androgenetic hair loss may benefit from medical therapies designed to reduce ongoing follicular miniaturisation and preserve surrounding native hair.
medical.examplesLead: Examples that may be discussed with a qualified doctor include:
medical.explanation: These medications may reduce the effects of DHT-related hair loss progression and help stabilise existing hair density over time.

natural.title: Natural Hair Preservation Support
natural.intro: Some patients explore natural support therapies as part of a broader long-term hair preservation strategy.

examples.2: Iron deficiency correction where clinically indicated

natural.explanation: Supporting scalp health and correcting nutritional deficiencies may contribute to maintaining healthier long-term hair quality in some patients.

regenerative.title: Regenerative Hair Support Options

intro.pre_surgery: Some patients explore supportive regenerative therapies as part of long-term maintenance planning before or alongside surgical treatment.
intro.post_surgery: Some patients choose supportive regenerative therapies following transplantation as part of long-term maintenance planning.

regenerative.explanation: These therapies are sometimes explored to support scalp health and improve the environment surrounding existing native hair follicles.

monitoring.title: Monitoring Future Hair Loss Progression
monitoring.intro: Even when transplanted grafts remain permanent, surrounding non-transplanted native hair may continue progressive thinning over time.

examples.4: Progressive donor reserve depletion if future surgery is considered

monitoring.explanation: Ongoing monitoring may allow earlier intervention before significant visible progression occurs.

longTermPreservation.safety: Before beginning any medication, supplement, or regenerative treatment, these options should always be discussed with your GP, prescribing doctor, or qualified treating clinician. HairAudit provides educational guidance only and does not prescribe treatment.

reviewInputs.title: What We Reviewed
reviewInputs.subtitle: HairAudit independently processes multiple forms of clinical information when generating your assessment. The following data sources contributed to this report.
reviewInputs.summary: This report was generated using multiple layers of procedural review, image analysis, clinical context, and long-term restoration modelling.
reviewInputs.summarySecondary: HairAudit independently reviews each submitted case using structured clinical assessment protocols.

items.clinical_notes: Clinical notes and context supplied by patient
items.future_risk: Future hair loss progression factors reviewed

assessmentConfidence.title: Assessment Confidence
assessmentConfidence.helper: This score reflects the completeness and usefulness of the submitted evidence. It does not represent a diagnosis, treatment recommendation, or guarantee of surgical outcome.

high.pre_surgery: This pre-surgery planning assessment had strong evidence coverage, including multiple planning views and relevant case information.
high.post_surgery: This post-surgery assessment had strong evidence coverage, including multiple submitted images, procedural details, and key donor or recipient views.

moderate.pre_surgery: This pre-surgery planning assessment had useful evidence coverage, but some planning views or supporting details were limited.
moderate.post_surgery: This post-surgery assessment had useful evidence coverage, but some views or supporting details were limited.

limited.pre_surgery: This pre-surgery planning assessment was completed with limited evidence coverage, so suitability and planning observations should be interpreted cautiously.
limited.post_surgery: This post-surgery assessment was completed with limited evidence coverage, so some image-based observations should be interpreted cautiously.

assessmentImprovement.title: How To Improve Assessment Accuracy
assessmentImprovement.subtitle: Additional information may help improve the depth and completeness of future HairAudit assessments.
assessmentImprovement.footer: Improved evidence may allow more detailed procedural analysis and more accurate long-term planning observations.
assessmentImprovement.highCoverageMessage: Your submitted evidence provided strong assessment coverage and allowed a comprehensive procedural review.

items.additionalPhotos: Upload additional photos from multiple scalp angles.
items.donorPhotos: Include donor area photographs showing the back and sides of the scalp.
items.crownPhotos: Provide top-down or crown photographs to allow better density and progression assessment.
items.recipientCloseup: Provide close-up recipient area photographs to improve placement and density review accuracy.
items.graftCount: Provide approximate graft count information if known.
items.procedureType: Share your procedure type (such as FUE, DHI, FUT, or repair surgery) if known.
items.procedureTiming: Include information about how long ago your procedure was performed.
items.patientNotes: Include additional background information about your concerns or surgery history.
items.supportingDocuments: Upload supporting documents such as clinic procedure summaries if available.

futureHairLossRisk.title: Future Hair Loss Progression Risk
futureHairLossRisk.subtitle: Hair transplantation restores existing hair loss, but surrounding non-transplanted hair may continue changing over time depending on genetic, age-related, and long-term progression factors. This section helps estimate whether future monitoring or preventative planning may be beneficial.

low.pre_surgery: Current visible patterns suggest relatively stable long-term hair characteristics for planning purposes, though periodic monitoring remains valuable.
low.post_surgery: Current visible patterns suggest relatively stable long-term hair characteristics surrounding your transplant, though periodic monitoring remains valuable.

moderate.pre_surgery: Some visible or clinical factors suggest future hair loss progression may continue over time. Ongoing monitoring and preventative planning before surgery may be beneficial.
moderate.post_surgery: Some visible or clinical factors suggest future hair loss progression may continue over time. Ongoing monitoring and preventative planning may help protect surrounding native hair.

elevated.pre_surgery: Several current factors suggest future hair loss progression may remain active over time. Long-term candidacy planning and preventative discussions with your clinician may be worth considering.
elevated.post_surgery: Several current factors suggest future hair loss progression may remain active over time. Long-term planning and preventative discussions with your clinician may help preserve transplant aesthetic balance.

hero.title: Your Pre-Surgery Review is Complete
hero.subtitle: We have independently reviewed your hair pattern, donor area, restoration suitability, and long-term treatment considerations. This report is designed to help you make a more informed decision before pursuing treatment.

scorecards.title: Planning assessment scorecards
scorecards.subtitle: Key metrics from your independent pre-surgery planning review.

sections.title: Independent planning sections
sections.future_progression: Future Hair Loss and Preservation Planning

images.title: Clinical Evidence Reviewed
images.subtitle: The following submitted images were processed and reviewed as part of your independent procedural assessment. This assessment incorporates all uploaded visual evidence used during procedural analysis.

trust.title: Independent Review Completed
trust.body: This assessment was generated through an independent review process designed to help patients understand their hair restoration options before making treatment decisions.
trust.neutrality: This report is not a clinic sales recommendation. It is intended to support informed decision-making.

nextSteps.title: Recommended Next Steps
nextSteps.subtitle: Practical actions to help you feel protected before committing financially.

hero.title: Your Post-Surgery Audit is Complete
hero.subtitle: We have independently reviewed your transplant result, donor condition, density patterns, procedural integrity, and long-term restoration concerns. This review is designed to help you objectively understand your surgical outcome.

repairStates.significant_planning: Significant repair planning may be beneficial

scorecards.title: Procedural assessment scores
scorecards.subtitle: Key metrics from your independent procedural review.

concerns.title: Potential Concerns Identified
concerns.subtitle: Objective observations based on your uploaded images. These are not a diagnosis — discuss them with your treating team.

images.title: Clinical Evidence Reviewed
images.subtitle: The following submitted images were processed and reviewed as part of your independent procedural assessment. This assessment incorporates all uploaded visual evidence used during procedural analysis.

trust.title: Independent Review Completed
trust.body: This assessment was generated using an independent review system designed to evaluate procedural quality objectively and separately from clinic recommendations.
trust.neutrality: This report is not influenced by the clinic that performed your surgery.

nextSteps.title: Recommended Next Steps
nextSteps.subtitle: Practical actions based on this independent review.

deleteDraft.confirmNamed:
Delete this draft audit (“{{title}}”)?

This will remove its answers, photos, and saved progress.
deleteDraft.confirmUnnamed:
Delete this draft audit?

This will remove its answers, photos, and saved progress.

completion.title: Case Completion
completion.subtitle: Based on photos + procedure + handling + healing inputs.

hliGuide.bodyLocked: Submit your free case to access the Long-Term Hair Restoration Guide from Hair Longevity Institute.
hliGuide.bodyUnlocked: Your case has been submitted. Download the Long-Term Hair Restoration Guide or read it online from your dashboard.
hliGuide.successLine: The guide becomes available after your submission.
hliGuide.hliAttribution: Guide authored by Hair Longevity Institute (HLI) for patient education.
hliGuide.boundaryNote: HairAudit runs your independent audit and review. HLI provides the guide only; it does not change audit findings.

unlock.sectionSubtitle: The more context you provide, the clearer and more helpful your review can be.
unlock.radarDesc: A balanced view of how your procedure appears across key clinical areas.
unlock.donorDesc: Review of extraction pattern, donor preservation, and long-term donor safety.
unlock.viabilityDesc: How graft care during surgery may relate to growth and viability.
unlock.aestheticDesc: Assessment of hairline design, direction, and overall visual balance.
unlock.timelineDesc: Stage-aware guidance on what to monitor as your result matures.

whyMatters.title: Why this matters
whyMatters.subtitle: Better details help us give you clearer, more useful findings.
whyMatters.donorRiskDesc: Helps reviewers assess donor safety and extraction patterns.
whyMatters.survivalDesc: Helps reviewers understand how grafts were cared for during surgery.
whyMatters.projectionDesc: Helps reviewers interpret your healing stage and what to monitor next.

caseHistory.title: My audit requests
caseHistory.subtitle: Your cases and audit status history.
caseHistory.empty: No cases yet. Create your first one to activate the dashboard.

graftIntegrity.rolloutBody: This feature is rolling out in your environment. Check back soon.
graftIntegrity.title: Graft Integrity Index™
graftIntegrity.subtitle: Claimed vs visually supportable graft ranges (probabilistic).
graftIntegrity.aiDisclaimer: This is a visual estimate based on your photos and is not a definitive graft count.
graftIntegrity.evidenceLimited: Evidence is currently limited. Adding clearer donor/recipient photos helps narrow the range.
graftIntegrity.emptyState: No estimate is available yet for your latest submitted case. Once enough donor/recipient photos are available, the system will generate a conservative range with explicit limitations.
graftIntegrity.pendingReview: Your estimate is being prepared and reviewed. You’ll see audited ranges once review is complete.
graftIntegrity.needsEvidenceBody: Upload clearer donor/recipient photos to narrow the range and increase confidence.

questionsPage.title: About your procedure
questionsPage.subtitle: These questions help us understand your surgery, healing, and results.

intake.introLine: About 5–6 minutes. Your answers help us prepare a thorough, independent review.
intake.advancedBody: Adds optional details about graft handling, donor area, healing, and aesthetic balance. You can skip anytime.
intake.lockedBanner: Case submitted. Your answers are now locked.
intake.photosSectionBody: Pre-procedure, surgery, and post-procedure images go in the next step.
intake.minimalMissingRequired: Please answer the required questions to continue.
intake.minimalContinueBody: Next, tell us where to send your report.

reviewSummary.title: Review Summary
reviewSummary.hint: Review your answers below. Use the step dots above to edit.

adv_basics.title: Basics
adv_basics.description: Optional. Helps reviewers understand your background and healing stage.

adv_hair_biology.title: Hair & Biology
adv_hair_biology.description: Optional. Improves progression and donor-risk inference.

adv_procedure_details.title: Procedure Details
adv_procedure_details.description: Optional. Improves technician-role and planning inference.

adv_graft_handling.title: Graft Handling
adv_graft_handling.description: Optional. Strongly improves desiccation and viability inference.

adv_healing.title: Healing
adv_healing.description: Optional. Improves stage inference and complication risk framing.

adv_current_status.title: Current Status
adv_current_status.description: Optional. Improves aesthetic consistency + patient experience context.

procedure_type.help: Choose what you were told; 'Not Sure' is okay.

would_repeat.prompt: Would you repeat the procedure with this clinic?

autoimmune_conditions.placeholder: e.g. alopecia areata, lupus, psoriasis (if known)

reports.subtitle: Your completed audit reports. View or download each report below.
reports.empty: No completed reports yet. Submit a case to generate your first audit report.
reports.shareHint: You can share this report with your clinic or specialist.

doctor.workspaceSubtitle: Complete the surgery form and upload photos per case. Each case uses the same audit flow: form → photos → submit.
doctor.noCasesBody: Begin building your verified professional profile by contributing your first case.
doctor.profileActiveTitle: Your doctor profile is now active.
doctor.profileActiveBody: You can begin building your verified record.
doctor.onboardingPageSubtitle: Set up your professional profile so we can attribute your cases and build your transparent record.
doctor.onboardingSubmitCta: Open case and submit for audit
doctor.checklistSubtitle: Follow these steps to complete your first case and contribute to transparent benchmarking.
doctor.whyMattersBody: Participation in HairAudit helps benchmark outcomes and improve transparency. Your documented cases contribute to evidence-based recognition and help set clearer expectations for patients and peers.

publicProfile.title: Public profile settings
publicProfile.description: Profile and visibility settings for how you appear on leaderboards and in the directory are coming soon. Your submitted cases already contribute to benchmarking.

training.title: Training portal
training.description: Targeted modules to improve documentation and audit readiness are planned. For now, use the audit feedback on each case report to strengthen your submissions.

defaults.title: Surgical defaults
defaults.description: Saving default answers (e.g. extraction method, holding solution) to reuse across cases is planned. For now, set values per case on each case’s doctor form.

reports.title: Audit reports hub
reports.description: A single place to see all your audit reports and pending actions is coming soon. For now, open any case from your overview to view its report and resolve feedback.

upload.title: Bulk upload
upload.description: A dedicated upload wizard for adding multiple cases in one flow is planned. For now, create cases from your overview and complete the form and photos for each case.

forms.onboardingLead: A minimal profile helps attribute your cases and build your public record.
forms.afterSaveHint: After saving, you can create your first audit case from the doctor overview and start building your evidence-based record.
forms.goToOverviewCreate: Go to overview and create a case →

page.title: Surgery Submission / Case Audit
page.description: Target 6–8 minutes. Complete as the treating physician.

extraction_devices_used.help: Select all that were used during this surgery.

clinic.overviewSubtitle: Operational trust and clinical quality across your Invited Contributions (cases you were invited to) and Submitted Cases (cases your clinic created).
clinic.profileActiveTitle: Your clinic profile is now active.
clinic.profileActiveBody: You can begin privately with internal audits.
clinic.noCasesBody: Start your first internal audit to begin building your clinic profile.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Portal configuration and default operational controls.
settings.recommendation: Settings will support role permissions, default visibility behavior, and white-label mode controls. You can already control case-level visibility from Invited Contributions.

benchmarking.title: Benchmarking
benchmarking.subtitle: Clinic and doctor benchmarking intelligence.
benchmarking.recommendation: This area will expose benchmark segments, confidence gating, and trend intelligence. Until this module is live, use the clinic leaderboard and Invited Contributions / Submitted Cases to monitor readiness.

training.title: Training
training.subtitle: Internal QA and training intelligence modules.
training.recommendation: This module will become your clinic's training hub, combining case intelligence, protocol adherence, and repeatable quality playbooks.

profileBuilder.trustSubtitle: A complete clinic identity plus deep procedural metadata improves attribution quality and reinforces medical credibility.
profileBuilder.basicSectionLead: This powers your trust posture and profile discoverability.
profileBuilder.saveOnceHint: Save once, then edit only what changed
profileBuilder.advancedSectionLead: Designed for QA, benchmarking, and training intelligence modules.
profileBuilder.clinicalStackLead: Add methods, tools, devices, machine details, optional extras, and protocols.
profileBuilder.capabilityPlaceholder: e.g. Sapphire FUE punch, Choi implanter, cold-chain protocol
profileBuilder.emptyGroupHint: No entries yet. Add structured details here to strengthen profile trust and improve benchmark-readiness.
profileBuilder.messageDefaultsApplied: Applied repeat-friendly defaults. Review and save.

upload.formatsHint: Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, and WebP. PDF where noted. Max 50 MB per file — large phone photos are resized automatically.

required.title: Photos we need

recommended.title: Extra photos (optional)

recommended.description: Helpful extra angles — never required to continue.

optional.title: Optional photos

optional.description: Only if you already have them — never required to continue.

progress.optionalAvailable: {{count}} optional photos you can add if you have them

messages.unsupportedFileType: Please choose a photo from your phone or computer.

messages.replaceFailed: Could not replace photo. Please try again.
messages.deleteFailed: Could not remove photo. Please try again.
messages.caseLocked: Your case has been submitted. Photos can no longer be changed.

card.qualityAdvisoryPrefix: This photo may be a little unclear.
card.qualityAdvisorySuffix: You can still add it — a sharper photo helps us review your case.
card.partialUploadError: Some photos could not be added:

completion.celebrationDetail: We have everything needed to begin your review.
completion.headline: Perfect.
completion.headlineDetail: We now have everything we need to begin reviewing your case.
completion.subcopy: You can continue now, or add extra photos if you would like to improve report accuracy.

optionalReveal.title: Extra Photos (Optional)
optionalReveal.subcopy: These photos are completely optional, but they may help us build a more complete review if you have them available.

extraPhotos.accuracyHint: Extra photos may help us better understand your situation.
extraPhotos.alreadyEnough: You already have enough photos to continue, but additional photos can sometimes help us build a stronger review.

wizard.heading: Your Independent Review Has Started
wizard.subcopy: We just need 5 quick photos so we can understand your situation clearly. Most people finish this in under 2 minutes.
wizard.completionSubcopy: We now have everything we need to begin reviewing your case. You are almost done — there are just a few quick questions before we prepare your report.
wizard.lockedMessage: Your case has been submitted. Photos can no longer be changed.

preSurgery.purpose: These photos help us understand your hair pattern and whether surgery might be a good fit for you.
preSurgery.confidenceMessage: Clear photos help us give you useful, easy-to-understand guidance.
preSurgery.requiredDescription: Five simple photos so we can see your hairline, sides, top, and back of head.
preSurgery.recommendedDescription: Extra photos that often help us give sharper advice — never required.
preSurgery.optionalDescription: Documents or references — only if you already have them.

preop_front.title: Photo Facing Forward
preop_front.description: Please take a clear photo facing directly forward. This helps us understand your current hair pattern.

preop_left.title: Left Side Photo
preop_left.description: Please take a photo of the left side of your hairline. This helps us understand recession patterns on the side of your hairline.

preop_right.title: Right Side Photo
preop_right.description: Please take a photo of the right side of your hairline. This helps us compare both sides evenly.

preop_top.title: Top of Head Photo
preop_top.description: Please photograph the top of your head clearly. This helps us understand thinning patterns on the crown.

preop_donor_rear.title: Back of Head Photo
preop_donor_rear.description: Please take a photo of the back of your head. This helps us understand the strength of your donor area.

preop_wet_top.title: Top of Head (Wet Hair)
preop_wet_top.description: Please photograph the top of your head with damp hair. This helps us see how much scalp shows through.

preop_hairline_closeup.title: Close-Up of Hairline
preop_hairline_closeup.description: Please take a closer photo of your front hairline. This helps us see fine detail along the hairline.

preop_donor_closeup.title: Close-Up of Back of Head
preop_donor_closeup.description: Please take a closer photo of the back of your head. This helps us see hair thickness in that area.

preop_clinic_quote.title: Clinic Quote or Plan
preop_clinic_quote.description: If you have a quote or treatment plan from a clinic, you can add it here. This helps us compare what was proposed with what your photos show.

preop_styling_reference.title: How You Usually Wear Your Hair
preop_styling_reference.description: A photo showing your usual hair length or style helps set realistic expectations after surgery.

preop_family_pattern.title: Family Hair Loss Pattern
preop_family_pattern.description: If relevant, a photo showing hair loss patterns in your family helps us discuss long-term outlook.

postSurgery.purpose: These photos help us understand how your transplant looks today and how the back of your head is healing.
postSurgery.confidenceMessage: Current photos help us review your result and give you clear, practical guidance.
postSurgery.requiredDescription: Five simple photos of your result, transplanted area, top, and back of head.
postSurgery.recommendedDescription: Photos from surgery day or early healing — helpful but never required.
postSurgery.optionalDescription: Clinic documents or milestone photos — only if you already have them.

preop_front.title: Front Photo
preop_front.description: Please take a clear photo facing directly forward. This helps us understand your current transplant result.

current_recipient_closeup.title: Photo of Transplanted Area
current_recipient_closeup.description: Please take a close photo of the area where your transplant was performed. This helps us review how evenly the hair was placed.

preop_top.title: Top of Head Photo
preop_top.description: Please photograph the top of your head clearly. This helps us understand your current hair coverage.

preop_donor_rear.title: Back of Head Photo
preop_donor_rear.description: Please take a photo of the back of your head. This helps us review the area where donor hair was taken.

preop_donor_closeup.title: Close Photo of Donor Area
preop_donor_closeup.description: Please take a close photo of the back donor area. This helps us review healing and donor appearance.

day0_recipient.title: Surgery Day — Transplanted Area
day0_recipient.description: If you have one, a photo from surgery day showing the transplanted area helps us see how hair was placed.

day0_donor.title: Surgery Day — Back of Head
day0_donor.description: If you have one, a photo from surgery day showing the back of your head helps us see how it looked right after the procedure.

postop_wet_recipient.title: Wet Hair Over Transplanted Area
postop_wet_recipient.description: Please photograph the transplanted area with damp hair. This helps us see coverage more clearly.

any_preop.title: Photo from Before Surgery
any_preop.description: A before-surgery photo helps us compare where you started with how things look today.

postop_day0.title: Early Healing Photos
postop_day0.description: Photos from the first few days after surgery help us see how things settled early on.

graft_count_board.title: Clinic Document (Graft Count)
graft_count_board.description: If your clinic gave you a graft count or similar document, you can add it here for context.

preop_clinic_quote.title: Clinic Quote or Treatment Plan
preop_clinic_quote.description: Your clinic plan adds helpful context for what was promised versus what your photos show.

postop_day1_recipient.title: Day 1 — Transplanted Area
postop_day1_recipient.description: A photo from the day after surgery helps us see early healing on the hairline.

postop_week1_recipient.title: Day 7 — Transplanted Area
postop_week1_recipient.description: A photo from about one week after surgery helps us track early recovery.

postop_month3_front.title: Month 3 — Front View
postop_month3_front.description: A front photo around three months helps us see early growth and recovery.

postop_month6_front.title: Month 6 — Front View
postop_month6_front.description: A front photo around six months helps us see how your result is developing.
